facts.value:
Jens Nielson
Written by his daughter Ane Kjerstine Nielsen Poulsen Christensen


April 2nd 1945
     My father Jens Nielson was born Oct 12th 1822 in Engager, Denmark. I understand he was raised by foster parents. His parents died when he was small. He was married and his wife died when his baby son was born. He brought his baby with him when he sailed for America but the baby died en-route and was buried at sea. He was a convert to the Church.

     He fought in the Civil War from start to the finish. He was wounded in battle. When he reached Ephriam, Utah with a company of immigrants he met the girl who later became his wife and my mother. She was among the groups of people who came to welcome the company.

     He had many trades. He raised hemp and made his own rope. He made adobe and built his own house in which he put a stone floor in the kitchen and the floor was white stone and smooth as oilcloth. He made a fireplace in the kitchen and it had a beautiful mantle shelf. He built a bake oven for the fire place. It set in the back of the fireplace and held 6 or 8 loaves of bread. Mother baked lovely cakes and bread in her bake oven. The cellar opened off of the kitchen and was as clean as could be it also had a brick floor. The walls were lined with adobe’s.

     Father’s barn was clean enough to live in. I could milk the cow dressed in my best and not get my dress dirty.

     He was a shoemaker by trade so he made all the shoes his children wore. He had twelve children so it took time to keep his own family in shoes but he made shoes for the public. He farmed 80 acres of land. He had an orchard and raised grain, potatoes and corn. He cut the grain with a cradle and taught us children to tie it in bundles. He raised dry peas and threshed them himself.

     There was one thing he wouldn’t do and that was butcher a pig. He always hired that done. The butcher would do the job for him for fifty cents or three ribs. However he did butcher his own sheep and cattle.

     Father was deeply religious. We had our family prayers morning and evening.

     In the early days in Utah the men folks had to take turns guarding the settlement from hostile Indians. Father had many experiences with the Indians but Mother had the most frightening encounters with them when they came to our home. But that is her story.

     With all the many other activities and responsibilities father had, he somehow found the time to cut the neighbors hair. Often this job had to be done for someone on Sunday mornings.

     Sunday afternoons after church we always had a crowd of young people at our home. Father was often the life of the party.

     Many times I’d play my accordion and he would sing. Then we’d have group singing and games. Father always joined in the games both indoors and out. Father could jump higher than any of the boys could. He was a first class marksman with a gun. Father had pretty good health all his life only his wound he received during the Civil War, until shortly before he died when he fell through the loft in the barn. He didn’t live long after that. He died at his home in Ephriam, Utah surrounded by his wife and all his children the afternoon of April 14th 1884. He was conscious up to the last.

     He lived all his life in the house he built for his first home. Both he and later his wife died in this home.

Note: Jens served as a Private in the Utah Territory Militia during the Blackhawk War. Jens helped in the construction of the Manti Temple. Many such as him would tithe time in its construction. He was buried in the Ephraim Pioneer Cemetery in Section 16. His headstone is broken or missing. Most of the headstones were made of sandstone and are in a bad state of preservation. Ane Kjerstena his second wife is also buried in the Ephraim Pioneer Cemetery. The location of her grave in the cemetery in currently unknown.

facts.place: Lewisville Cemetery, Lewisville, Jefferson County, Idaho, United States - Plot: Block 1-Lot 32-Plot 8

facts.value:
The Life Summary of Marie Magdalena Nielsen
When Marie Magdalena Poulsen (Nielsen) was born on 28 March 1868, in Ephraim, Sanpete County, Utah, United States, her father, Jens Nielsen, was 45 and her mother, Ane Kjerstine Andersen, was 31. She married Christian Poulsen on 1 March 1882, in Salt Lake City, Salt Lake County, Utah, United States. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 6 daughters. She lived in Warm River, Fremont County, Idaho, United States in 1910. She died on 13 March 1934, in Lewisville, Jefferson County, Idaho, United States, at the age of 65, and was Lewisville Cemetery, Lewisville, Jefferson County, Idaho, United States - Plot: Block 1-Lot 32-Plot 8
